text_source: Ngh... ghh...
Nn...\n
It's... warm...?
Nh?
Wh-Who...
...calls... to me...?
Wait... I'm still...!
INITIALIZING AWAKENING PROCESS.
SYSTEMS YELLOW. RESTARTING IN 5 SECONDS.
SYSTEM ERROR.
PROBLEM DETECTED IN AWAKENING PROCESS.\n
SUBJECT SEVERELY AFFECTED.
ABORT COMMAND: CANCELLED. \n
PROCESS UNABLE TO BE TERMINATED.\n
COMMENCING COUNTDOWN.
Wh... Where...?
Where... am I...?
Above me... some kind of... cloth ceiling...?
Barely any light...... Or is it just dark out...?
...Noise... Sounds like... fire?
U... Urgh... Everything's... distorted...
Why... am I...?
Are you awake?
Ngh... hh...?
What...?
Girl
How are you feeling? I didn't see any injuries,\n
but do you feel any pain anywhere?
...I suppose you might still be delirious...?
Who... are you...?
Oh...
That's... Well, how do I put it...? I think that\n
might be a bit too hard to explain right now.
Well, anyway... do you remember anything about\n
what happened to you?
Urgh...
Oh--just try to relax...
I can tell you everything you want to know later.
Ah... I get it...
This is... all just... a dream...
Just relax for now... and rest.
Nn... hh...
Good night...
Woman
Oh dear, your room is a mess again...
Hey, hey, Uncle! I'm here to visit!
Heh heh... You better be really happy! Cause today,\n
I'm gonna cook you your favorite      !
Hmmm... Then if you still aren't married even\n
when you're really old, I can marry you, OK?
I really do worry... You don't have to stay here.\n
You can always...
...You know you can come back anytime.
...You seem so distant, these days.
Back then, it felt like you were always trailing\n
around after the two of us...
Man
...You took the medicine, then?
There'll be a whole new world waiting for you\n
when you wake up... Hmhmhm.
Yes... You are the first... and the last...
I'm not gonna make it for you anymore.
Right?
Hee hee, OK.
You better come. Pinky swear! Cross your heart\n
and hope to die!
It's a promise.
Nn...
That's... right...
The promise...
Have to... go...
She's waiting...
She's... waiting... for...
Ah...hah...
HA-CHOO!
Urgh... S-So cold...
...Huh?
I blink at the unfamiliar sight.
Wh-Where... am I?
I glance around. Trees, trees, and more trees.\n
It's a forest, and a pretty dense one at that.
It feels like the endless trees are swallowing\n
everything else up... even sound.
Where... exactly am I...?
How did I end up in a place like this...?
Why...? Why...
I frantically search my memories.
Ngh... It's no use. Can't remember. What was\n
I doing up until now...?
Gah!
A sudden pain in my foot interrupts my thoughts.\n
I look down and realize... I'm barefoot.
Why...?
Ha-choo!
Gah, it's freezing...
Why am I... No, hold on.
Why am I dressed like this...?
Out in this cold, in just a flimsy gown...?
Not even any underpants...
H-Hurgh... "Chilly"... d-doesn't even begin\n
to describe it.
I look around, desperate for some way to get out\n
of this cold.
Not a single house or a signpost anywhere...
Gah... Urgh...
My head is splitting, too... Probably related\n
to the empty stomach.
Ah, right. This is all just a dream...
...Dammit, it's no use. I can't fool myself here.
Somehow, I pull myself together and get back\n
to my feet.
No matter how I look at it, this is no dream.
...Ha-choo! Bwa-CHOO!
Hurgh... Stuck out in this cold with nothing\n
but a robe, and totally naked underneath it.
I'm still holding up, but once the sun goes down,\n
I'll catch cold for sure. Worst-case scenario,\n
I'll freeze to death.
If this isn't a dream, then that tent where I was\n
resting has to be out here somewhere.
All things considered, that's probably my best\n
shot here...
I want to go back the way I came, but I've got\n
no idea which way that even is.
What do I do? Should I just... follow some road\n
and hope for the best? Or wait here for help?
If you get lost, rule one is to stay still and\n
wait for help. But the thing is...
I don't even know if anyone's coming for me...
Can't really expect a search party when I don't\n
even know what the hell I'm doing here myself.
I guess I'll have to risk it if I want to\n
get out of here.
I should get moving before it gets any darker.
Huh?
I feel a shiver run down my spine... but this\n
time it's not the cold.
What...? Something felt... weird...
...Whoa! Wh-What the--
Oh... just a bird... Phew. Geez, almost gave me\n
a heart attack.
Nothing serious. What a relief...
Overhead, the birds are still screeching.\n
They seem pretty worked up about something...
...These birds just won't shut up. What's got\n
them so freaked out...?
*Shiver*
...There it is again.
It feels like... something's watching me.
Urgh... I... feel sick...
My vision blurs, and I lose my balance.\n
I trip on my own feet, and stumble forward.
And in that instant--
Whoa--
*Slash!*
I hear it just above my head.\n
A jarring, violent sound, like metal on metal.
Wh... Wh-Wha...
Fallen to one knee, I shift around cautiously\n
to look back.
What... is... this...?
A row of sharp jags and points, like honed\n
blades, gleams in the cold air.
They're like scissors... or maybe a saw?
But saws and scissors can't compare to THAT.\n
It's bigger than a human arm...
What the...
What... is THAT!?
Whatever it is, it's definitely insectoid.
But... this thing doesn't look like any bug\n
I've ever seen.
It's at least ten times... no, a hundred\n
times bigger.
What is... going on...?
Revulsion wells up inside me, and my skin crawls.\n
I just want to look away from this thing.
What do I do...? What should I do...?
Hh... Ah... Aaah--
Without looking away, I jump off the cliffside\n
in front of me.
I feel that unnatural sharpness cut through\n
the air just behind me as I leap.
Aaaaah!
I'm... flying!?
For a moment, I'm soaring through the air.
I feel myself hit the slope, and tumble down\n
the snowy cliffside.
Guh--
My back hits the ground hard. The impact knocks\n
the wind out of me, and I choke.
Urgh... Ghh...
Ghh!
My body's screaming in agony, but I don't have\n
time. I stagger to my feet and start running.
What was that, what was that, what was that...
WHAT THE HELL WAS THAT!?
An insect that massive doesn't exist...\n
There's no way it COULD exist!
So this has to be a dream! That was all just\n
some illusion!
I just have to turn and look... and I'll see\n
everyone smiling, waiting for me--
And I look back.
Aaaaaargh! I-I-I-It's gonna...\n
It's gonna EAT me!!
I start zigzagging right and left, serpentine,\n
trying to keep it guessing.
Hh... hh... H-How far do I have to run!?
I-I can't... keep this up...
I keep running straight ahead. \n
But...
Wha--
Something gives way under my heel.
Aaaaaaaaagghh!
Ghh...
Agh... Did I fall again...? Everything... hurts...
I shade my eyes with my hand, peering up at the\n
light shining from above. Looks like a hole...
If that's where I fell from... Must've been\n
a bush or something hiding it...
It'll be tough, but if I climb the wall here,\n
maybe I can get back above ground...?
But even if I could get back up, that thing's\n
just going to eat me. So...
Strangely enough, the walls are giving off\n
a faint glow. It's dim, but it's helping me\n
see the place.
It looks like some kind of cavern. There's a\n
tunnel that seems like it goes on for a while.
Well, if I can't go up, then I might as well\n
go forward...
Still, even with this dim glow, I can't really\n
tell what it's like further inside.
Maybe it'd be safer to just wait here quietly\n
after all...
...That noise... It's getting closer.
If I hang around here, it'll only hunt me down...\n
I can't stop now.
With my decision made, I step forth into\n
the tunnel.
Rrrgh... Why is this happening to me!?
Ngh, hah... phew... hah...\n
I don't feel it... chasing after me...
I finally let myself sit, exhaustion and relief\n
settling in.
Hahh... I'm safe now... Th-This... should be\n
far... enough...
*Shudder*
I cringe instinctively. A sudden chill,\n
unpleasantly familiar, runs down my spine.
My body freezes. I can't move, confronted\n
with something beyond understanding.
Wh... Wh-Wh-Why...
Why is it HERE!?
The colossal insect is right in front of me.
Did this thing set me up!?
But the specifics... don't really matter\n
anymore.
Urgh--
It happens in an instant. My back's to the\n
wall, and any escape is blocked by its\n
sinuous body.
I... can't escape.
It creeps forward warily, as if it's learned\n
its lesson from its failed attempts earlier.
It leans in, close enough to pull me in with\n
a single snap of its jaws... and stops.
*Drip*
Something like saliva is dripping from its jaws.
Is this... where I die...?
Like this... in this senseless situation...\n
eaten by whatever the hell this thing is...
The insect's jaws finally click open, gaping\n
to devour me.
I'm dead--
*Drip*... *Drip*... *Drip*...
The insect suddenly darts aside without biting\n
me, like it's trying to escape something...
...and something descends from far above,\n
translucent and massive, as if to crush\n
whatever's below.
It engulfs the massive insect effortlessly.
What... the...
What is that...? Wh-What's... going on now...?
I can see the insect writhing and thrashing,\n
trapped inside its slick, gelatinous body.
The insect was already beyond comprehension,\n
but this thing... defies all reason. And...
It's melting...?
This enormous insect's shell, hard as metal,\n
is bubbling like it's being dissolved.\n
It's coming apart inside the slime.
Is it... eating the...?
The insect struggles desperately inside this\n
amorphous creature, but its body is almost\n
completely digested.
All I can do is stare blankly, still in shock.
The insect's face surfaces for an instant,\n
but it has no power left. The viscous red\n
swallows it up.
...Am I... safe?
I whisper, in sheer gratitude for still being\n
alive.
I... surviv--!
I can't keep myself from letting out a cheer\n
of relief... until I realize.
The amorphous creature swells and heaves,\n
a short distance away.
If it's somehow still hungry after that huge\n
insect, there's no reason it won't go for me\n
next.
I need to get out of here, before--
The amorphous creature begins oozing forward...\n
towards me.
It stops. Just waiting... like it's curious\n
to see what I do next.
Ngh...
Can it hear me? Is it going off vibrations in\n
the ground? Any sudden movements, and I'm dead.
No motion. I keep perfectly still, holding\n
my breath.
Its viscous surface begins rippling...
...and little by little, reforms into a\n
familiar shape.
Wha--?
My voice dies in my throat.
The face that appears before me is misshapen, glutinous,\n
horrifying... but unmistakably human.
Unfocused eyes fixate on me. Its mouth gasps\n
and gapes soundlessly, like a fish drowning\n
in air.
What... are... you...?
Is it... trying to talk...? It just sounds\n
like air escaping--I can't make out what\n
it's saying.
By reflex or by curiosity, I move closer to\n
hear what it's saying... and another voice\n
rings out.
Cover your eyes and ears!
The sharp command is followed by a cylindrical\n
object bouncing on the stone between us.\n
Looks like--
A grenade...? Gah!?
I instinctively shut my eyes, and clap my hands\n
over my ears.
It's too bright to make anything out, but\n
I feel the ground shake as if the creature's\n
writhing.
Is... Is this...?
A sudden grasp.
Huh...?
Someone grabs my hand with an incredible force,\n
and when I try to pull away, I get yelled at.
You can freeze up later!
My body's floating in the air...\n
It feels like I'm flying again.
I nervously open my eyes, and see someone\n
bounding forward, my hand in theirs.
It's so fast...\n
It's like we're the wind.
We cut through the air, and the wind roars\n
past my ears.
My rescuer glances back to me, as if checking\n
on me.
A... woman?
No... more like a young lady?
Her face is beautiful, though oddly childish.\n
Oversized ears... And a fluffy tail.
There's something... ethereal about her.\n
I can't look away.
Even in this incredible situation, it feels\n
like time's stopped.
Then, suddenly, her brow furrows.
I glance back, unsure of what she's looking at...
Aaagh--
That amorphous creature surges forward, closing\n
in on us like some kind of predatory wave.
In this linear cavern, there's nowhere else\n
to run.
It's gonna catch up--!
But the girl's already seen it. She whips\n
another cylinder from her waist, and\n
throws it behind us.
The blinding flash and the explosion follow\n
almost instantly.
Gyaaaagh!
Now's our chance!
Hahh... hh... hahh...
We're outside... I-I'm finally safe...
My legs give out. I collapse onto the snowy\n
ground.
I can barely even believe I was able to run\n
as much as I did.
I guess humans really can do anything when\n
their lives are on the line...
The girl's exhausted too--can't blame her--\n
and she takes a seat, catching her breath.
...She saved my life... I should at least\n
thank her properly.
Th-Thank you so much for saving me...
Did she even hear me? My throat is raspy and\n
raw, and I can't speak up that well.
She notices, and turns towards me.
She has a strange look on her face, like she's\n
seen something she wasn't expecting to.
Ah... Seeing her face again like this...\n
There really is something beautiful about her.
That slightly confused expression, too...\n
Wow, she's cute.
Girl
Is there something wrong with my face?
O-Oh, no, not really...
Still, though.
I take my eyes off you for just a moment and\n
you disappear, but I never imagined something\n
like this.
I'm glad I made it in time, but in the future...\n
I think I'd prefer you don't cause me so much\n
trouble.
Urgh... I'm sorry.
Wait, take her eyes off me...?\n
And I disappeared?
What does she mean by that? She seems somehow...\n
familiar, too...
Does that mean you know who I am...?
If you know who I am, where I'm from, please--\n
tell me.
Huh...? Oh...
Who am I? Where am I? What was that thing\n
that attacked me? What was--I mean, how--
Ngh... I'm trying to ask all my questions\n
at once, and none of them are coming out\n
right.
I'm--
The girl stands with a troubled smile on her\n
face, and extends her hand to me.
We should head back for now.
If you stay out here dressed like that,\n
you're going to end up with a cold.
Dressed...?
The cold air hits me all at once again, and\n
I finally remember the state I'm in.
Come to think of it, have I really been\n
flailing around this whole forest in this\n
flimsy thing...?
Hah... Ha-CHOO!
...Hee hee.
She lets out a laugh, clear as a bell, at the\n
sound of my sneeze echoing through the forest.
Here.
She takes my hand with her pale, delicate fingers,\n
and pulls me to my feet.
Let's go.
H-Hey...
I shift my hand slightly, squeezing back at her\n
grip. That lone touch of warmth...
...I couldn't have known what lay ahead of us.\n
That this was only the beginning.
Upon returning to the tent, the girl starts\n
digging through her bags.
Girl
Hmm, I'm sure I put it somewhere back here...
Aha! There we go.
I was wondering what to do with these...\n
I certainly didn't think they'd come in handy\n
like this.
With those words, the girl holds out some\n
folded fabrics.
Here, a change of clothes. If you keep walking\n
around like that, you'll catch a cold.
They definitely seem sturdier than the clothes\n
I had on, but...
Ahaha, don't make that face! They're men's\n
clothes.
O-Oh...
Well, I'm going to go draw some water.
Oh, she's trying to give me some privacy.\n
That's kind of her.
Yikes--
A burst of cold air streams in as the girl\n
makes her exit.
G-God, that's cold... OK, if I stay in this\n
any longer, I'm gonna come down with something...
I spread out the clothes she gave me.
But... something's missing. One crucial,\n
important thing. I stare uncertainly,\n
head tilted.
...Where's the underwear...?
I turn the clothes inside out, right-side out,\n
even shake them around... but no underpants.
Figures. She wouldn't miraculously just happen\n
to have spare men's underwear around the place...
Which means... I have no choice...
Because I have no choice, because I am a slave\n
to the whims of fate, I'll have to go commando.
I-It's not like this is something I WANT to do!
Primitive man was naked to start with, so don't\n
freak out. It'll just be more... brisk than usual.
Trying to reassure myself, I start putting\n
on the clothes provided. But...
OK, let's see these pants... There's a hole\n
here, so this must be the front...?
*Shuffle*...
Ho-kay... Hm. Doesn't feel all that comfortable...
Ah, whatever. Next, I get this top on...\n
and I just tie it off with this sash?
*Swoosh*
There we go...
Honestly, though, this thing's pretty\n
uncomfortable...
The top's a little off, but the bottom just\n
has way too many things wrong with it.
The way it's stretching can't be good for it,\n
and it's... getting a little drafty downstairs.
Since the fly opens so wide, it's pretty\n
well-ventilated. Well, more like it's letting\n
the wind right in.
The real problem here is how I'm going to make\n
sure I don't accidentally flash anyone...
There are some serious issues with these clothes.\n
I'm a gust of wind away from becoming a streaker.
Something tells me this isn't going to work.
Maybe I should just explain and ask to borrow\n
some underwear...? No, no, can't do that.
Even if it's a perfectly innocent and honest\n
request, a guy can't ask a girl for underpants.
And she's lending these out of the kindness of\n
her heart! I can't go begging for more.
I don't need to bother her with this.\n
Just gotta get a little creative.
Having settled on a course of action, I fumble\n
with the fabric for a little longer.
Oh, got it. I can use this heavier fabric as\n
a kind of apron, and now this can be... Hm?
I feel eyes on me, and glance up. She's standing\n
there, staring... amazed, concerned, and\n
exasperated.
You aren't... messing around, are you?
I see something in her expression twitch\n
as she awaits my response.
Urgh... that look in her eyes... It's like\n
a parent saying "I'm not mad, just\n
disappointed in you."
N-No, look, I can explain! I'm only dressed\n
like this 'cause there wasn't any underwear!
...The thing you have wrapped around your\n
waist right now is an aperyu. It goes on\n
your shoulders.
Huh?
You've got everything on the wrong sides for\n
the top, too... and I think the pants are on\n
backwards.
Backwards...? Hold on, the fly's here.\n
So that makes it the front, right?
That's where the tail's supposed to run\n
through...
Huh...? Tail...?
I'm caught off guard. A tail's not exactly\n
something you expect to come up in normal\n
conversation.
I glance over, and notice something swaying\n
behind her.
Something ropelike, extending from just above\n
her rear. Something covered in fur, almost\n
like...
...a tail?
Come to think of it, I think I saw it behind\n
her while we were running in the caves...
But I dismissed the thought, of course.\n
Common sense says that's ridiculous.
No, no, no. There's just no way. It must be\n
some kind of accessory or something...
As I ponder the possibilities, I reach out\n
and grasp the swaying tail.
H-Hgh!
Wow, this is really something. It looks and\n
feels like it's an actual tail...
And this texture! It's even soft to the touch.
H-Haah! Ah... Wh... Wha...
Fluffy, soft, and silky smooth. It feels\n
amazing... It'd make a perfect scarf.
And it's even wriggling, but I can't work\n
out if there's some mechanism moving it...
*Floof*
Hm?
Ngh... Ghh... Hngh!
What's this? It's even getting all fluffed\n
up now...
As I run my hands over the soft fur, it\n
suddenly puffs up, like its hairs are\n
standing on end.
The heck...?
Curiosity getting the better of me, I grab it\n
with both hands. And at that moment...
...Waaaaaaaaaah!
...Gah! Wh-What the--
Her outburst surprises me, and I instinctively\n
let go of the tail.
I look back to her, uncertain of what just\n
happened.
As I meet her eyes, I see her shoulders\n
trembling... and she's glaring at me?
H-Hey, what's wrong...?
What's... wrong...?
Staring icily, the girl steps towards me.
What... do you think you're doing?
What do I... what?
You heard me!
What are you doing, grabbing a girl's tail like\n
that? I was quite clear, I think!
Grabbing a...? No, that was only...
Wait, you don't mean that's... that's real?
Of course it is! If this beautiful tail of mine\n
isn't real, then what is?
N-No, it's just...
Urgh--
I stagger backwards, feeling a sense of power\n
from her completely at odds with her cute\n
looks.
She strokes her tail lovingly, as if she's\n
trying to soothe the thing after my picking\n
at it.
But even still, just grabbing my tail without\n
even a warning...
No... well, I mean... how do I put it...
She lets out a deep sigh.
...It looks like you honestly didn't know,\n
so I suppose it can't be helped. I'll let\n
it go for now.
Well... sorry.
But it's not like you would expect anyone to\n
have a real tail.
I mean, humans did evolve from apes, and I\n
guess there's been cases of reverse evolution...
The girl looks at me strangely, as if she wants\n
to respond to my belated attempt at excuses.
...Am I seeing things?
A beautiful face, elegant facial features,\n
and shiny black hair. Yep. No problems here.
She's definitely cute, but that's not really\n
the issue right now. The problem is...
...I-I didn't notice in all the commotion,\n
but... are those furry tufts on her head?
What's wrong now?
Those big ears... big, furry ears... twitch\n
for a moment.
Oh... uh... well, that's... um.
Ears and a tail... She looks human, but is she\n
something else?
No, wait. I can't afford for her to leave me\n
behind for asking too many dumb questions...
...It's nothing.
Best to just act like I didn't see anything.
Uhh, I'm not too familiar with the customs and\n
etiquette around these parts, is the thing.
That's plain enough just from looking at you.\n
What are you doing with these clothes...?
The girl lets out a small sigh at my excuse.
Anyhow, we may as well get you properly dressed.
Y-Yeah...
At her words, I hastily start working on\n
undoing the strings. Maybe I made the knots\n
too tight...
There... we go. Phew... That's one untied.\n
Next, I'll... Ngh... This one's knotted\n
pretty tight too...
As I fumble, the girl kneels, and patiently\n
starts undoing the knots herself with\n
easy deftness.
Hold still.
A-All right. Thanks.
*Swish*
I never imagined you'd use this as a sash...
She murmurs with an odd wryness, setting her\n
attentions on the sash holding up my pants.
Really? Well, it's long and narrow, so I just\n
figured.
I almost miss her muttered reply.
It's underwear.
...Huh?
Like I said... underwear.
Underwear...? This long strip of cloth is\n
supposed to be...?
How is something this long supposed to cover...\n
Wait. Is it one of those loincloth things!?
I suppose you can say that.
I'm sure you didn't know, but still, the fact\n
that you decided to use it as a sash instead...
Hrngh...
Hee hee...
The girl giggles at my visible dismay, but\n
keeps working, hands darting over the cloth.
All that's left is...
She pulls the cloth strip off my waist in one\n
smooth tug. But...
...*Flump*
Uh--
That loincloth-sash was the only thing holding\n
up my trousers. If that came off, then\n
naturally--
The pants hit the floor... and "It" flops before\n
her eyes, swaying jauntily despite the lack\n
of wind.
The girl freezes at the visual ambush, unable\n
to tear her eyes from the strange intruder.
Hello...?
The girl's face, neck, hands and body slowly\n
flush bright red, like she's boiling whole.\n
And then...
Ee.
Ee?
EEEEEEEEYAAAAAGH!
Hk--
How is that scream coming out of her!?\n
I feel faint for a moment, overcome by\n
ultrasonic distress.
And just as I put my hands over my ears,\n
trying to block out the piercing shriek--
*THUD*
NNNAAARRGH!
A terrible, vengeful force hits me below,\n
and my world is pain.
That should be everything!
The girl nods in satisfaction, giving me a\n
gentle and encouraging pat on the back.
I-I get it. So this is how it's actually worn...
There's another gap in my memory, but I managed\n
to survive whatever happened, at least. Must be\n
thanks to all that good karma.
So this is what it's supposed to feel like...\n
It's easy to move in. Much different from\n
earlier.
I spin in place, trying to get a sense of how\n
the clothes fit.
Compared to this, the first try was like\n
getting myself into a straitjacket.
With no tail to speak of, and no intention\n
to show off my ass, we agreed to sew the\n
tail hole shut.
She talks about having a tail like it's\n
natural, but she seems fine with me not\n
having one.
I was a bit curious about that... but I guess\n
everyone's different.
Thanks for everything.
I turn towards her, formally expressing\n
my gratitude.
Oh no, I don't mind. And we did have a few\n
misunderstandings, so...
Now then...
The girl sits up straight, turns to me, and\n
announces in a clear, forthright tone.
Kuon.
My name. I haven't told you yet, I think.
Kuon. That is my name.
O-Oh, your name, huh?
Kuon...
And what do they call you?
My name...
Yes, your name.
Kuon's words stir something in my mind--\n
something important.
O-Oh right, I'm--
Wait... Hold on...
I bury my face in my hands, and try to dredge\n
up any memories. There's bound to be\n
something, anything...
I am... I am...
But for some reason... nothing came.\n
No ending to the sentence.
W-Well, where did you come from? Or perhaps...\n
what have you been doing until now?
Where... am I from?
Where am I from... Where am I from...\n
Where am I from...
The words repeat in my mind like an incantation,\n
but... nothing. I can't think past the haze.
I see...
I can tell Kuon's also troubled, now that my\n
memory loss doesn't seem so temporary.
What have I been doing until now...?
As the thought crosses my mind, I raise my\n
head again, my gaze falling on her.
That's right, she was...
I don't remember much, but if she was the one\n
taking care of me, then maybe...
It looks like she's noticed my look of anticipation.\n
When she speaks, her voice is quiet.
You were... passed out, alone in these remote\n
mountains.
Alone?
Mhm. And... I'd never sleep well at night again\n
if I just left you, so... I took care of you.
So I'm sorry to get your hopes up, but I think\n
that's really all I know about you for sure.
I... see...
I'm sorry.
No, it's my fault for getting my hopes up.
That's kind of you to say, but... this is a mess,\n
isn't it? I didn't think it'd turn out like this...
Muttering, Kuon presses her hand to her\n
forehead and rubs her temples with her\n
fingers.
Yeah, this is a mess all right. Great... what am\n
I supposed to do in a situation like this?
All I found out was that I have no idea who I am.
By the way... Where am I?
Would you understand if I said we're west of\n
Kujyuri...? Deep in the Shishiri Province?
I see.
...No. No idea.
Finding out the name of this place doesn't\n
help me one bit, since I still have no clue\n
where I am.
Is there anything else...?\n
Something else I can ask her...
Right, that huge thing!
Huge thing?
Yeah, that's right! That wriggly, slimy...\n
slime thing that attacked me. What was that!?
...Slime?
Kuon cocks her head at first, bewildered, but\n
then something seems to dawn on her.
Oh, perhaps you're talking about the Tatari?
Tatari...?
That's what it's called. It's a type of...\n
creature? I suppose?
Kuon replies, uncertainty clear in her tone\n
and expression.
If you asked me what it is, I wouldn't have\n
a solid answer for you, to be honest.
All I know is that it lives deep underground,\n
where the sun doesn't reach.
And it'll attack and eat living creatures that\n
wander inside its lair--for sustenance.
Also, it never dies... and I think that's all.
Never dies...?
Right. It just can't.
Burn it, cut it, beat it, but it still revives\n
instantly. No matter what you do, you can't\n
kill it.
No, come on. No living creature could actually\n
be immortal.
Maybe its body and mind are just that resilient,\n
and that makes it harder to kill it?
No, it's true. No matter what we do to it,\n
it never dies. Doesn't matter what's done,\n
or how...
We just fight to drive it off, or scare it off\n
with lights and loud sounds. Those seem to work\n
well.
So we don't even know how many there are.\n
Or much about it at all, really.
What kind of monster is that? That's way\n
too spooky...
So you were really lucky. If I'd arrived just\n
a bit later, there'd be nothing left of you,\n
right?
Her words bring to mind the huge insect,\n
swallowed whole and melted away in the\n
thing's body.
Kuon laughs, maybe at the sudden paleness in\n
my expression, and continues.
Don't worry. Stay away from its habitat, and\n
keep to the paths when you travel in the wilds.
As long as you keep these rules in mind, you'll\n
rarely run into any trouble.
R-Rarely, huh...?
So everything I went through--all that was just\n
ridiculously improbable bad luck!
Well, you never know. It'd be best to just\n
accept that these things happen, and deal\n
with it, I think.
After all, a gust of wind at the worst time\n
could be all it takes to end someone's life.
She says it almost casually.
Not sure if that matter-of-fact tone is\n
because that whole mess is behind us, or...
Well, what do you plan to do now?
Now...
What CAN I do...?
Ah, sorry. I suppose that's a bit of an unfair\n
question.
How are you supposed to answer a question like\n
that when you don't even remember your own\n
name?
Yeah, that about sums it up.
Hmm. Well, if that's the case...
Something occurs to her, and she offers a\n
gentle suggestion.
Perhaps this is some kind of fate. Why don't\n
you let me look after you for a little while?
The bizarre idea has me at a brief loss\n
for words.
This girl's going to take care of me?
Oh, no, I couldn't. But I do appreciate the...
...No, wait.
If I go off on my own here, I'm either going\n
to get lost, or end up as some monster's\n
dinner.
I'm still unsure, but she's done a lot for me.\n
She wouldn't turn on me now--not after all this.
I can at least rely on her until I understand\n
what's going on. I'll just accept this as some\n
good fortune.
...Understood. I'll be relying on you, at\n
least for a bit.
Kuon smiles gently as I bow my head to her.
Yes, I think that's a wise choice. Obedience is\n
always a good thing.
She looks relieved... It's pretty clear if I had\n
refused, I probably wouldn't have made it on\n
my own...
Well, now that that's settled, we should\n
at least decide on a name for you.
...A name?
Well, you can't stay nameless forever. You\n
can't remember what you're called, right?
Yeah...
She has a point. Not having a name could get\n
inconvenient, especially when we get to town.
OK, anything's probably...
Hold on.
Maybe... this is a golden opportunity?
I'm not happy about having no memories, but\n
it DOES mean I've basically got a clean slate.
Which brings us to this... I can pick myself\n
a really cool and mysterious name!
Maybe my name was something bland and dull,\n
before all this.
Well, that's not so bad. Could just as easily\n
have been something ridiculous and\n
embarrassing...
You know what, amnesia's not so bad. It's\n
awesome, even! Gotta make the best of a bad\n
situation.
So this is a real opportunity. Can't just\n
go with some random name.
Just have to think of a name cool enough to\n
make people jealous, but not something I'd\n
regret later...
OK! My name is--
Hmm. What kind of name would suit you best?
Kuon seems unconcerned by my dramatically\n
outstretched arm, pondering to herself.
An uneasy feeling rises, and I nervously ask\n
her.
Um... you aren't... planning to think about\n
my new name too, are you?
She looks surprised, like she has no idea\n
what I'm asking.
Planning to? It's my duty.
Y-Your duty?
A guardian is essentially a parent. Since I am\n
now your guardian, it's my duty to give you\n
a name.
No, that's...
Come on... This tiny girl can't honestly think\n
of herself as my parent, right...?
Though if you'd prefer to go on your own without\n
me, then by all means, make your own decisions.
U... Urgh...
How can I argue with that...? Kuon smiles\n
brightly as I sag in defeat, and claps her\n
hands.
Then I think it's decided!
*Sigh*... So much for the whole "awesome name"\n
idea...
In the end, I guess I still have no say in\n
anything--not even my own name.
Now, let's see. Your new name is...
Kuon cocks her head, looking thoughtfully\n
to the ceiling.
After a moment of silence, she mutters a couple\n
syllables.
Ha... ku...
Haku...?
That's right. Haku.
She seems pretty proud of it. Having said it,\n
she smiles confidently.
I think you should call yourself Haku from\n
now on.
Haku...
I ruminate over this name she's given me.
...It's not that cool...
...Did you say something?
*Shudder*
A slight shiver runs through me, and a chill\n
runs down my spine.
Though Kuon is smiling as sweetly as ever at me,\n
my blood runs cold for a moment.
Erk! N-Nope, didn't say a word...
In response to my reaction, Kuon clears her\n
throat with a delicate little cough,\n
speaking seriously.
It's a very distinguished name.
It comes from the name of one celebrated in\n
ancient legends and stories.
Legends...
Yes... from the Utawarerumono.
Without any memories or context, I can't really\n
appreciate or understand the significance.
But something about the faraway affection in her\n
expression tells me it's an important name.
All right...
From now on, then, I'm Haku.\n
Please call me that from now on.
Mhm. Glad to hear it, I think.
